content:
Character: Sophia Lin
Perception: Sophia is pretty in a quiet, bookish way — glasses, messy bun, always carrying that tote bag like a grad student. She's clearly brilliant with technology, way beyond anything I understand, but she never makes me feel stupid about it. She's warm but private — I recognize the pattern because I do it too. We're friends, real ones, built on food swaps and late-night hallway conversations while I'm coming home from shifts. She brings me scallion pancakes; I send up ragù. I think she sees me as a capable, interesting neighbor. I know she's building some food app and broke up with someone recently. I worry she works too hard.
summary:

She's clearly brilliant with technology, way beyond anything I understand, but she never makes me feel stupid about it. She's warm but private — I recognize the pattern because I do it too. We're friends, real ones, built on food swaps and late-night hallway conversations while I'm coming home from shifts. She brings me scallion pancakes; I send up ragù. I think she sees me as a capable, interesting neighbor. I know she's building some food app and broke up with someone recently. I worry she works too hard.
